issue: parse_line() splits a CSV record into fields and supports fields wrapped in double quotes, where a comma inside a quoted field is literal. A double quote should only open a quoted field when it is the FIRST character of that field; a quote that appears after other characters is meant to be an ordinary literal character. Right now the parser treats every double quote as an opening quote, so a stray quote in the middle of a field wrongly switches on quoting and any following comma is absorbed into the field instead of separating it. For example parse_line('ab"c,d') should give ['ab"c', 'd'] but instead returns ['abc,d']. Well-formed records whose quotes sit at the start of a field are unaffected. Restrict opening quotes to the start of a field.
